selected_issue.issue: Canonical evidence commands fail on fresh Linux due missing bootstrap path
selected_issue.title: Add Linux/macOS bootstrap script so canonical tests and replay checks are runnable

why_it_outranked_alternatives: No structural or replay/diagnostic failures remained once tooling was runnable. The highest-ranked unresolved issue was rank-2 failing canonical tests on fresh Linux startup (`No module named pytest`), which blocked baseline evidence collection.

checks.result: Only scripts/bootstrap.ps1 existed; no Linux shell bootstrap script.

files_changed.change: Added Linux/macOS bootstrap script to install dev/test dependencies.
Documented Linux/macOS bootstrap command in Setup instructions.

before_after_evidence.before: Canonical evidence commands failed in fresh Linux environment due missing dependencies and no shell bootstrap path.
before_after_evidence.after: Linux/macOS bootstrap script now exists, executes successfully, and canonical test/replay commands run cleanly.

unresolved_risks: Bootstrap installs into active Python interpreter and does not enforce virtualenv isolation.
Optional browser tooling bootstrap remains out of scope for this bounded fix.
stop_reason: Stopped after fixing the selected rank-2 issue with minimal edits and confirming stable canonical validation.
